finding: 4-node repro: {Camera (factoryID=23) + 'Frame B' framing behavior (factoryID=3) + 'Source' layer + 'Transition B' scenenode (factoryID=11)}. All 14 Replicator/Cell tiles were REMOVABLE without losing the bug -> the divergence is NOT the tile wall, it is the FRAMED-CAMERA POSE of a single framed layer (confirms T-H2/S6 resolveFramedWallPose anchor bug). Per-frame engine-vs-FCP: f00-f07 both black (match); f08-f19 FCP=black(0.0) but ENGINE renders B at mean 34.3 (engine brings framed B on-screen ~12 frames too early, at a wrong static pose); f20-f23 FCP reveals B (mean 51->119, the real Frame-B framing ramp) while ENGINE stays FROZEN at 34.3 (mis-posed, static, never matches the reveal). Net: engine paints one static off-centre framed 'Transition B' the whole clip instead of FCP's black-then-framed-reveal schedule. Bug lives in the framing pose/schedule for the 'Frame B' behavior (factoryID=3) + Camera, i.e. evaluator/framing.ts resolveFramedWallPose anchor (bottom-edge [2399,-2145] instead of content centroid) AND its reveal timing, not the replicator grid.
gate_result: FIX-FRAMING (2026-07-14): resolveFramedPose single-behavior reveal+cover fix. Min-repro engine-vs-FCP 8.14 -> 84.42 dB (f08-f19 now black=99dB matching FCP; f20=12.7, f22=9.8, f23=13.8 reveal-ramp). Full GUI-GT gate `fct regress engine` = 0 regressions / 0 improvements (baseline_engine byte-identical). tsc clean, no-hardcode policy PASS. NOTE: the FULL Video_Wall slug (2 factory-3 behaviors) uses resolveFramedWallPose (unchanged, still 9.10 dB) — this fix corrects the SINGLE-framer reveal path exercised by the minimized repro; the wall-pose anchor/schedule for the full slug remains a separate open item (T-H2/S6).

fix_2026_07_15_M_VIDEOWALL.note_vs_fix_framing: COMPLEMENTARY to FIX-FRAMING above: FIX-FRAMING fixed the SINGLE-behavior resolveFramedPose path (the minimized 1-behavior repro -> 84.42 dB). THIS change fixes the FULL slug's 2-behavior resolveFramedWallPose path (wall anchor + animEnd), taking the full Video_Wall slug 9.10 -> 10.24 dB. Both land together; no overlap (different framing pose functions).
changes: parser/index.ts: framing-scene animEnd = MAX Framing-behavior <timing out> (Frame B 1.9686s), not the normalized 0..1 stroke keyframe (was 1.0, truncated the dolly)
evaluator/index.ts: computeWallCenter (bbox centre of the LARGEST replicator) + pass as wallCenter to resolveFramedWallPose
framing.ts: wallCenter param anchors the wall pose on the tile-wall centre instead of the ray-cast corner (2399,-2145)
fix_2026_07_15_M_VIDEOWALL.gate_result: 0 regressions vs baseline_engine (tol 0.3dB); tsc clean; no-hardcode green; baseline re-frozen
fix_2026_07_15_M_VIDEOWALL.remaining: The wall dolly still frames ~1 tile through mid/tail (GT reveals the full 3x3 grid); far-dolly distance + proxy->content reveal SCHEDULE (per FIX-FRAMING's decode: black f00-f19 then ramp) remain for the 2-behavior wall path.

decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.root_cause_correction: DEEPER than the recorded finding (which blamed a mis-posed framing pose). The engine's timeMap.remap() COLLAPSES ALL scene times to 0 for Video_Wall: buildTimeMap sets wrapSec=0.367s because Transition A (drop zone, retimingExtrapolation=1 'wrap') times out at 0.367s, and the retime-wrap loops the playhead back to frame-0 past that. But the Video_Wall transition is authored by the CAMERA DOLLY (two Framing behaviors running to animationEnd=1.969s), NOT the drop-zone crossfade. So every rendered frame evaluates at t=0 -> one static pose. Frozen-at-0 coincidentally scores ~10.24 (holds GT near-tile f0 look); unfreezing (cancel wrap for framing scenes) drops to ~9.0 because the framing pose + wall placement are ALSO wrong.
decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.decoded_schedule: GT near->far->near dolly: f0=one tile (photo A near); f4-f16=dolly OUT revealing full ~3x3+ tile grid (tiles crossfade A->B); f20-f23=dolly IN to one tile (photo B near). framer window [0,0.9676] pulls OUT, Frame B window [0.8676,1.9686] pulls IN. Peak-far f~11.6.
decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.decoded_geometry: AOV=45. content tile 1920x1080 scale1.01 -> near cover ~1317, near fit ~2341. Framer PROXY small shape (half 186.8x254.5) at world Z=5711; framePose(proxy) eye ~Z5109 in front of wall (Z~0), where wall half-width ~4100 fills 45deg frame (35.7 vs 36.4 horiz half-AOV) => full-grid reveal. farDist should be proxy-eye dist ~5109, NOT single-tile fit ~2341 (current code barely dollies).
three_parts_needed_together: 1. timemap.ts: cancel drop-zone wrap when scene has Framing behaviors (factory 3). hasFramingCamera() helper. Fires only Video_Wall (0.367<<end) + Clone_Spin (1.869~=end, negligible).
2. framing.ts resolveFramedWallPose: far=proxy framePose eye dist from wall anchor; near=content cover; schedule=near->far->near triangle over the two windows.

3. WALL PLACEMENT (real blocker): 14 replicators must populate a DENSE grid filling the frame at far. Engine renders ~5 sparse tiles on black (means ~30-50 vs GT ~90-130). Pin-1 has 3x3(9) at origin; 13 others carry 1-3 edge/fill tiles at (2000-6000). Verify all instances render + resolve A/B cell content + land on-screen through the oblique camera.
4. camera orientation: proxy fwd=(0.069,0.422,0.904) tilts view (real - GT tiles show 3D perspective). Keep wall centered/on-screen (tiles slid off in WIP).
decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.status: All 4 must land TOGETHER to net-improve; each partial regresses because frozen-at-0 accidentally scores 10.24. Parts 1+2 verified correct in isolation. Part 3 (dense wall placement) is the remaining blocker. Reverted this tick per Rule 2d; decode preserved for the multi-tick build.

decode_2026_07_15_timing_gate.correction_to_part3: The '~5 sparse tiles / dense wall placement blocker' framing in decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze part 3 is REFINED: the 14 replicators are each TIME-GATED with a STAGGERED timing window [in,out] (measured, in seconds): #1 Pin2c[0.63,1.13], #2[0.63,1.13], #3[0.50,1.84], #4[0.03,0.77], #5[0.00,0.53], #6[0.40,0.97], #7 Pin2[0.00,0.37], #8[0.90,1.77], #9[0.50,1.77], #10[0.73,1.77], #11[0.60,1.77], #12[0.60,1.87], #13[0.53,1.87], #14 Pin1[0.00,1.10]. At any instant only the replicators whose window contains t are visible (op=1); the rest are op=0. This is the INTENDED staggered wall build-up (tiles pop in over time), NOT a placement/density bug. At t=0.5 (mid), 4 replicators are live (Pin1 9inst + 3 others 4inst = 13 instances); combined with the correct far dolly ~18/32 project on-screen. So part 3 is really: (a) the wrap-cancel (part 1) so t advances and the staggered windows actually fire, (b) the correct near->far->near dolly + wall-centered anchor (parts 2+4) so the live tiles land on-screen. The tiles crossfade A->B via their Pin1(=A)/Pin2(=B) cell sources (resolveCellImage already maps Pin1->imageA, Pin2->imageB). NET: the subsystem is wrap-cancel + 3-key anchor dolly + correct wall centroid; the replicator content/timing is already parsed correctly. The remaining risk is purely the CAMERA PATH (anchor + dolly schedule), not tile generation.

build_attempt_2026_07_15_3key.result: NET-NEGATIVE, reverted per Rule 2d (gate: 1 reg Video_Wall 10.24->9.52, 0 improvements, all 64 others byte-identical). WIP saved: docs/notes/wip/vw_{framing,index,timemap}_3key.ts.txt.
what_worked: timemap wrap-cancel for framing scenes (hasFramingCamera): time now ADVANCES (was frozen at t=0) — objectively correct, the staggered replicator windows now fire.

3-key anchor camera path in resolveFramedWallPose (key0=near-A static-cam pose, key1=far proxy-eye depth @ wall centroid, key2=near-B content framePose): the NEAR-B END NOW SETTLES ON PHOTO B correctly (GT f23 match, was black/wrong before). Per-tile A/B also renders (Pin1->A sepia, Pin2->B blue mix visible mid).
computeWallCenter changed to all-instance centroid: mid tiles better centered (8.9->9.46).
remaining_gap: MID-WALL still too SPARSE + tiles too small vs GT's full tight grid. Root: at t=0.5 only 13/32 instances live (correct staggered timing) but they span a HUGE ±5000 area (Pin-1 3x3 alone is 8200x2400 wide) so each tile projects tiny; GT shows a COMPACT grid filling the frame. Either (a) FCP frames a TIGHTER region (just the Pin-1 3x3 center, not the scattered decorations at 2000-6000), or (b) the far dolly distance is too large. farDist=proxyEyeDist~5109; wall-fit(Pin-1 halfW4100)=~5496 similar. The scattered replicators at 2000-6000 may be OFF-screen decorations FCP doesn't frame.

f0 near-A renders FLAT SEPIA (solid color, no photo detail) — the near-A key frames staticCamPos XY (2051,-2390) at nearDist but the A tile there shows one flat color; needs investigation (is the standalone Transition A tile rendering its photo, or a placeholder, under the framed camera?).

NEXT: (1) try framing ONLY the Pin-1 3x3 grid (main wall) — exclude the scattered 2000-6000 replicators from the wall centroid + use Pin-1 extent for farDist; (2) debug f0 flat-sepia (A-tile content under framed cam).

build_attempt_2026_07_15_3key_v2.result: Reconstructed the 3-key camera path + wrap-cancel; iterated to 9.94-9.98 (best), still < frozen baseline 10.24. NOT shipped (net-negative + best result needs a fitted far-distance constant = fit-scaffolding).
confirmed_wins: NEAR-A key now works: f0 = 18.20 dB (was ~8, flat-sepia). Fix: near-A eye = staticCamPos.xy at (contentPlaneZ + nearDist), look straight down at the A tile plane (NOT the static camera's own Z=23 which is a rig depth, far too close).
NEAR-B key works: f23/f21 settle on photo B (~10.3 dB, was black).
all-instance wall centroid (1016,1903) beats Pin-1-only origin centroid (9.57 vs 9.07) — the scattered 2000-6000 replicators ARE part of the visible wall.
build_attempt_2026_07_15_3key_v2.far_distance_finding: CRITICAL: the proxy-eye far distance (~5109) is TOO FAR — the mid-wall reveal zooms out so much that only 3-4 of ~13 live tiles fall in the framed view (41-66% of the frame is BLACK) while GT shows ~9-12 tiles filling the frame. Sweeping a far-distance multiplier FCT_FARK: 1.5->9.50, 1.0->9.57, 0.6->9.71, 0.4->9.84, 0.3->9.94, 0.25->9.98 (peak), 0.20->9.97, 0.15->9.93. So the true far distance is ~0.25x the proxy-eye depth (~1280, barely dollying out). The proxy-framePose-eye model for farDist is WRONG; the real far pose frames the compact visible wall tightly, not the proxy's far Z. But even the tuned peak (9.98) is < 10.24, and 0.25 is a fitted constant (fit-scaffolding, banned).
build_attempt_2026_07_15_3key_v2.remaining_gap: Even at the optimal zoom the mid-wall has fewer tiles than GT (~4 vs ~9-12) because the live instances at t=mid are spread over ±5000 while GT's visible wall is a compact cluster. The far distance must be DERIVED from the actual visible-tile bbox extent (frame the live-instance cluster tightly), not a constant and not the proxy depth. NEXT: compute the bbox of currently-LIVE (op>0) replicator instances per frame and set farDist = fit-that-bbox-to-frame; that removes the fitted constant AND matches GT's tile density.
build_attempt_2026_07_15_3key_v2.reverted: per Rule 2d (net-negative) + no-fit-scaffolding (the 0.25 peak is a fitted constant). WIP not committed to engine; full spec here + docs/notes/wip/README.md.

decode_2026_07_15_ROOT_CAUSE_replicator_.finding: The Video_Wall mid-wall blocker is NOT the camera path — it is the REPLICATOR INSTANCE LAYOUT. Exhaustive measurement of the camera far-distance across 3 models: (a) proxy-eye depth ~5109 -> 9.57 (too far, 40-66% black); (b) FARK sweep of proxy depth peaks at 0.25x -> 9.98 (fitted constant, still < 10.24); (c) live-instance-bbox fit-to-frame -> 9.23 (the live bbox spans +/-5000 incl. scattered decorations, so it over-zooms). ALL three plateau below the frozen 10.24. Root cause: the engine's replicator instances are SPREAD over +/-5000 (Pin-1 3x3 at origin spanning +/-4100, PLUS 13 scattered replicators at world 2000-6000), but the GUI GT's visible wall is a COMPACT cluster of ~9-12 tiles filling the frame. No camera framing derived from these spread-out instances can match GT, because the spatial DISTRIBUTION differs. Either FCP culls/repositions the scattered replicators, or the engine mis-parses their world positions (Pin behaviors / replicator origin offsets).
decode_2026_07_15_ROOT_CAUSE_replicator_.camera_path_status: SOLVED (validated): near-A key f0 8->18.2 dB, near-B settles on photo B, wrap-cancel unfreezes time. These are correct and reusable — the remaining work is purely the tile layout.
decode_2026_07_15_ROOT_CAUSE_replicator_.next_investigation: Compare the engine's replicator instance world positions against what FCP actually renders (GT). Specifically: (1) do the 13 scattered replicators (world 2000-6000) actually appear in GT, or are they off-screen/culled? (2) Is the Pin-1 3x3 spacing (sizeWidth=8200 over 3 cols = 4100 pitch, but tiles are 1920 wide -> big gaps) correct, or should tiles be larger/closer to tile densely like GT? GT shows tiles nearly TOUCHING (thin seams); the engine's 4100-pitch 1920-tiles have huge gaps. LIKELY: the replicator cell SCALE or the sizeWidth/Height (grid pitch) is mis-parsed — GT's tight grid implies pitch ~= tile size (~1920-2000), not 4100. Check replicator Size id parsing + cell scale.
decode_2026_07_15_ROOT_CAUSE_replicator_.verdict: Camera subsystem done; hand off to a replicator-layout investigation. Do NOT keep tuning the camera — the ceiling is ~9.98 and the gap is spatial.

build_attempt_2026_07_16_4part_T-qvidwal.result: NET-NEUTRAL (+0.02 dB), reverted per Rule 2d. All 4 parts implemented & verified in isolation. WIP diffs: docs/notes/wip/vw_T-qvidwall01_{timemap,framing,evaluator,compositor_cellfill}.diff.
build_attempt_2026_07_16_4part_T-qvidwal.gate: Behavior-NEUTRAL on all non-target slugs (serial JOBS=1 re-measure: Wipes__Mask 14.14=14.14, Center_Reveal 15.10=15.10, Slide 20.33=20.33). The `fct regress engine` gate under parallel JOBS=3 reported 3 regressions (Smear/Center_Reveal/Slide) + 1 improvement (Wipes__Mask +2.19) — ALL were RENDER-NONDETERMINISM ARTIFACTS: those slugs have NO replicators and NO framing camera so my changes provably cannot touch them; a serial re-render reproduced clean-main byte-for-byte. IMPORTANT META-FINDING: the engine render is non-deterministic at ~0.5-1dB under parallel JOBS; the gate must be run serially (or the noise floor raised) to trust <1dB deltas.
what_worked_verified: PART 1 timemap wrap-cancel: added `hasFramingCamera` scan (any camera layer with camera.framing.length>0), OR'd into the existing wrapSec cancel gate. Video_Wall wrapSec=0.367 << end=1.969 so it fires; unfreezes time (means now vary f0-f23, was flat 84.1). Clone_Spin also has a framing cam but wrapSec~=end so the `endSec>wrapSec+frameSec` guard no-ops it. VERIFIED: only the 2 framing-cam built-ins are eligible, every other slug's camera.framing is empty.

PART 2+4 3-key camera path in resolveFramedWallPose (added staticCamPos + frameWidth params): key0 near-A = look straight down -Z at content plane from staticCamPos.xy at nearDist (COVER fit, min(halfH/tanH,halfV/tanV)); key1 far = anchor + proxyFwd*farDist (proxyFwd=(0.069,0.422,0.904) gives the correct oblique perspective); key2 near-B = straight down at content(B) tile centre at nearDist. Ease-lerp key0->key1 over [0,proxyOut=0.9676], key1->key2 over [proxyOut,end]. farDist = |proxy framing-point - anchor| projected on fwd (~4362, no fitted constant). VERIFIED ENDPOINTS: f00 8->18.54 dB, f23 9->13.23 dB (both settle on the correct near photo). Timing decoded: proxy beh in=0 out=0.9676 (dolly OUT), content 'Frame B' beh in=0.8675 out=1.9686 (dolly IN). Motion Transition type=3.

PART 3 CELL-FILL (the real new finding): tiles must be scaled to fill their grid cell. Grid pitchX = sizeWidth/(cols-1) (main 3x3 = 8200/2 = 4100); raw 1920-px tile at 4100 pitch leaves ~2180px black gaps -> engine rendered ~4 tiny tiles on black at far dolly (41-66% black) while GT shows a frame-filling grid. Uniform cellFill = pitchX/tileWidth (~2.14x) applied to instTransform scale makes adjacent tiles meet -> mid frames fill with photo content. Gated cols>1 && fillScale>1 (fires on grid replicators only; dot/panel replicators keep scale 1). Fully derived from parsed grid geometry, NO fitted constant. Sweep: CELLFILL 0->9.06, 0.5->9.99, 1.0->10.18 (full-fill), + far-zoom FARK 0.4 -> 10.46 (but 0.4 is a fitted constant, banned).
build_attempt_2026_07_16_4part_T-qvidwal.why_blocked: Even with all 4 parts, Video_Wall = 10.18 vs 10.16 clean = +0.02 net (target >=13). Per-frame: ENDPOINTS jumped ~8dB each (f00 18.5, f23 13.2) but the DEEP-DOLLY mid frames f14-f17 DROPPED to 8.3-8.9 (was ~10 when frozen-at-near-A held the sepia opening). The remaining gap is the EXACT tile grid at the deep-far pose: GT shows a TIGHT REGULAR 4x4 grid of full 16:9 photo tiles with thin seams (see GT f11 full-res), but the engine's cell-filled tiles OVERLAP (full-cell-fill scales each tile to 4100 wide = 2306 tall, so they overlap vertically at 1200 pitch) and the 14 scattered replicators (main 3x3 at origin +/-4100 PLUS 13 fill/edge replicators at world +/-2000..6000) do NOT reproduce GT's regular grid positions/count. The endpoint gains (~+8dB x2/24 frames = +0.67) are almost exactly cancelled by the deep-dolly mid losses.
build_attempt_2026_07_16_4part_T-qvidwal.next_investigation: The blocker is now precisely the TILE GRID GEOMETRY, not the camera (camera 3-key + cell-fill are correct & reusable from the WIP diffs). Two concrete leads: (1) cell-fill should fill to a UNIFORM scale that keeps tiles TOUCHING WITHOUT OVERLAP - i.e. scale = min(pitchX/w, pitchY/h) would keep 16:9 aspect but under-fill X; the correct Motion behavior may be that the CELL itself is 16:9 sized to pitchY (1200 tall -> 2133 wide) and the horizontal pitch (4100) is a MIS-PARSE (should be ~2133 for touching). CHECK the replicator Size id=347 semantics: is sizeWidth the TOTAL span (span/(n-1)=pitch) or the per-cell size? If per-cell, pitch = sizeWidth/cols not /(cols-1). (2) The 13 scattered edge replicators at +/-2000..6000 may be OFF-SCREEN decorations FCP culls - verify which instances actually land in GT's frame at the far pose and whether the main 3x3 alone (excluding scatter) reproduces GT's 4x4 better.

decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.context: Continuation of T-qvidwall01. Restored the 4 WIP diffs (they were deleted by an unrelated Smear commit 86b8489), verified: apply cleanly + Video_Wall 10.16->10.18 (unchanged +0.02); reverted engine per Rule 2d.
decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.new_finding_1_perspective_ratio_refutes_: Measured GT f11 tile seams empirically: horizontal seams at x={17,605,1236,1883} (screen pitchX ~630 growing rightward from perspective), vertical seams at y={257,585,957} (screen pitchY 328-372 growing downward). Observed screen aspect pitchX/pitchY = 1.68. For Pin-1 3x3 (sizeW=8200, sizeH=2400) ALL three pitch models (span/(n-1)=4100/1200, span/cols=2733/800, span/(cols+1)=2050/600) give the SAME world ratio 3.42 -> screen ratio (after Y-tilt cos(25 deg)=0.906 foreshortening) = 3.77, not 1.68. **The pitch-model X-vs-Y ambiguity is NOT the root cause** -- Pin-1 3x3 sizeW/sizeH = 3.42 is too oblong to match GT's tight screen grid regardless of which cols-denominator we pick. Something else is driving the grid geometry.
decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.new_finding_2_scattered_replicators_are_: Pin-1 (3x3, +/-4100 wide, +/-1200 tall) alone cannot produce GT's ~4 visible rows tightly stacked. The other 13 replicators (Pin 2 copies, rows=1 lines at world Y +/-2000..+/-6000, sizeH=2400 but only 1 row so Y-pitch irrelevant) MUST contribute the extra rows. GT's 4-row grid is likely rows from Pin-1 (row 0 at y=0) PLUS 3 line-replicators at y=+/-1200, +/-2400. Their sizeW=8200-8260 matches Pin-1's 8200 -> they align vertically as a synthetic 4x3 grid. So the pitch is fine; the question is TIMING/VISIBILITY: which of the 13 line replicators are active + on-screen at t=mid.
decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.new_finding_3_standalone_transitionAB_re: The .motr scene has 'Transition A' and 'Transition B' as SEPARATE top-level Image scenenodes (ids 987619199, 987619197) in the 'Source' layer -- Type=1 (drop zone A) at world (2050,-2390,0), Type=2 (drop zone B) at world (2054,+3596,0), both 1920x1080 scale 1.01. The engine's compositor renders every Image with dropZone through projectFramed (compositor/index.ts:542). At the mid-far dolly this puts the raw Transition B at world y=+3596 through the wide-oblique framing camera and it lands as a GIANT solitary blue tile filling ~40% of the frame (visually confirmed by rendering engine f11 with WIP diffs applied: 7 sepia tiles from replicator cells + 1 oversized blue rectangle from raw Transition B). GT does NOT show this giant tile -- FCP suppresses standalone Transition A/B renders when they're consumed as replicator cell content, or renders them behind/culled. Pin 1 (id 988619845) and Pin 2 (id 988619875) ALSO exist as top-level Image scenenodes (Type=3 = cell content) but their world position is at origin -- through the framing cam they land in the SAME place as replicator cells, so they don't add a visible artefact. Transition A at Y=-2390 and Transition B at Y=+3596 do.
concrete_next_leads: L1 (highest ROI, generic): filter out standalone rendering of ANY top-level image scenenode with Object.Type in {1,2} (Transition A/B drop zones) when the scene has factory-3 framing behaviors AND >=1 replicator layer -- the drop zones are used as cell content sources, not visible in the wall view. Fires on Video_Wall + any other replicator-wall transition; a no-hardcode detector hasReplicatorWallWithDropZoneRefs should be added. Verify: rendering f11 without the raw Transition B should REMOVE the giant blue tile artefact.

L2: verify the 13 line-replicators positions -- Pin-2 copies with Columns=3, Rows=1, sizeW=8200-8260 are horizontal lines. If their world Y positions (Position.Y in each replicator's Transform) span -3600..+3600 or so, they form the extra rows GT shows. Would need to dump each replicator's Position param to confirm.
L3 (deferred): the pitch model may still be fine as-is (H_A: span/(cols-1)) -- the mid-frame 4-row grid is Pin-1's 3 rows + Pin-2's 3 lines at neighbouring Y, not a single 4x4 replicator.
decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.actionable_diff_shape: engine/src/compositor/index.ts around line 498 ('if (layer.type === image || layer.type === generator)'): add early-return when layer.source?.type in {transitionA,transitionB} AND rctx.framed AND rctx.hasReplicatorWallLayer (new capability), skipping the standalone drop-zone card render. Do NOT skip when transitionA/B is the ONLY visible content (regular crossfades).
decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.risk: Standalone-render skip could regress ordinary crossfades if the guard isn't tight enough. Detector must fire on >=2 built-ins (Video_Wall + one more replicator-wall transition). Verify no-hardcode test still green (all >=2).
decode_2026_07_16_T-qd1814800_transition.gate_status: Engine byte-identical to origin/main (WIP files-only commit). Full-slug serial gen -> fct regress engine = 0/0. WIP diffs re-verified apply cleanly against current main; the timemap diff still notes context drift from post-rebase.
